name: How long does a standard exterior door installation take?

mainEntity.name: How long should I expect a front door replacement to take, including cleanup and final adjustments?
mainEntity.text: How long should I expect a front door replacement to take, including cleanup and final adjustments?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard exterior door installation is often completed in a single day, but the actual time depends on the condition of the opening and the scope of work. If the new door fits the existing frame well, the installer may be able to remove the old unit, set the new one, seal it, and complete basic hardware adjustments in just a few hours. The job takes longer when the frame is rotted, the jamb is out of square, the threshold needs rebuilding, or the lockset and trim need custom fitting. Painting, staining, drywall repair, and inspection-related steps also add time. In coastal neighborhoods, extra attention to sealing and flashing can extend the process slightly, but that work is often worth it for long-term performance. Before hiring, ask for a realistic timeline that includes demolition, installation, weatherproofing, cleanup, and any return visit for final adjustments after the door settles.
acceptedAnswer.description: A straightforward exterior door replacement often takes a few hours, but the total time can stretch longer if the frame is damaged, the opening needs adjustment, or finish work is included. Custom doors, weatherproofing, and trim repairs add time. A reliable installer should give a start-to-finish timeline before work begins.
